title: The Planner in Mukalla
briefing: Intelligence has fixed Tariq al-Bakri, the architect of the Norfolk pier bombing that killed 17 sailors, in a crowded apartment block in the old quarter of Mukalla, Yemen. A drone window opens in ninety minutes, but the building is full of families. Take the shot and accept the collateral, or wait for a cleaner chance that may never come?

choices.label: Authorize the strike
choices.detail: Take al-Bakri off the board, accept civilian risk.
choices.result: The missile lands. Al-Bakri is confirmed dead, along with at least nine others, several of them children. Hawks call it justice; allied capitals from Berlin to Ottawa issue furious statements.

news.headline: President eliminates the Norfolk pier mastermind
Drone strike kills nine civilians to reach one man
Top terror planner killed in Mukalla strike
BOOM. POTUS DROPS THE HAMMER ON TERROR KINGPIN 💥🎯

social.text: al-Bakri built the bomb that killed my shipmates. no tears here.
those were apartments in Mukalla. families. we will be counting the dead for days.

choices.advisor: We get al-Bakri, but the footage of that building will run for a week.

choices.label: Hold for a cleaner shot
choices.detail: Protect the civilians, risk losing the target.
choices.result: You wave off the strike. Al-Bakri slips out of Mukalla with a supply convoy by nightfall and vanishes into the Hadhramaut. The families live; the planner walks.

news.headline: President spares a packed building, holds his fire
Hesitation lets the Norfolk planner escape
Strike called off over civilian concerns; target at large
POTUS BLINKS?? TERROR BOSS GHOSTS INTO THE DESERT 👻

social.text: a president who won't bomb a daycare to make a point. imagine that.

choices.advisor: Cleaner conscience, dirtier problem. Al-Bakri is still out there planning the next one.

title: Section Nine Sunsets
briefing: The warrantless bulk-collection authority known as Section Nine expires at midnight, and the agencies swear it is the backbone of every plot they have stopped. Civil-liberties groups call it a dragnet over 120 million innocent Americans. Renew the program intact, let it lapse, or rebuild it with a FISA-court warrant requirement nobody at Fort Meade wants?

choices.label: Renew it, no changes
choices.result: You sign the straight reauthorization. The server farms outside Bluffdale, Utah keep humming. Privacy advocates promise a Supreme Court fight you may not win.

news.headline: President keeps our best counterterror tool online
Mass surveillance of 120 million Americans renewed
Section Nine reauthorized ahead of deadline
BIG BROTHER GETS A SHINY NEW LEASE 👁️📡

social.text: the plots they stop never make headlines. this keeps us alive.

choices.advisor: Fort Meade is thrilled. The courts and the kids on the feed are not.

choices.label: Let it expire
choices.detail: End the dragnet, accept a blind spot.
choices.result: Midnight passes and the program goes dark. Privacy groups celebrate; the intelligence chiefs warn you just blinded the country.

news.headline: President lets the surveillance dragnet die
We just went blind, and the President let it happen
Section Nine lapses; agencies scramble to adapt
POTUS PULLS THE PLUG ON THE SPY MACHINE 🔌😱

social.text: fifteen years of fighting this and it just... ended. crying.

choices.advisor: Liberty wins tonight. Pray nothing slips through the new gap before the next briefing.

choices.label: Renew it, but require warrants
choices.detail: Threads the needle; angers the agencies.
choices.result: You sign a version with FISA-court warrants baked in. Reformers call it a landmark; the agencies call it a paperwork ambush that will cost them weeks on every target.

news.headline: President renews surveillance with new warrant rules
Hard-won warrant requirement reins in the dragnet
New red tape will slow agents chasing real threats
JUDGES NOW GUARD THE KEYS TO BIG BROTHER ⚖️🔑

social.text: FISA warrants plus the program. boring, smart, and somehow it passed.

choices.advisor: The compromise nobody loves and history might. The spy shop will grumble for months.

title: The Grid Goes Dark
briefing: A cyberattack traced to Zarya, a GRU-backed crew working out of Moscow, just blacked out four million homes from Cleveland to Buffalo and bricked Toledo's water utility for two days. Cyber Command has staged a counterstrike that could fry the grid around Rostov and Voronezh. Hit back in kind and risk a spiral, expose the Kremlin publicly and rally NATO, or absorb it quietly to avoid escalation?

choices.label: Launch the counterstrike
choices.detail: Hit their grid. Eye for an eye, online.
choices.result: Cyber Command pulls the trigger and half of Rostov goes dark for a night. The Kremlin goes quiet, then threatens retaliation. The cyber-cold war just got hotter.

news.headline: President answers a cyber strike with a knockout blow
Retaliatory hack risks a war nobody can see coming
U.S. hits Russian grid after blackout; tensions surge
POTUS HACKS BACK AND ROSTOV GOES PITCH BLACK 💻🌑

social.text: they turned off toledo's water. you do NOT let that slide.

choices.advisor: Deterrence restored, maybe. Or we just taught the Kremlin what counts as fair game.

choices.label: Expose them, rally the allies
choices.detail: Declassify the attribution, sanction together.
choices.result: You declassify the forensics and march NATO and the G7 to the podium. Moscow denies everything as joint sanctions land. Hawks grumble it was all talk and no payback.

news.headline: President names Moscow in grid attack, unveils coalition
Diplomatic answer avoids a dangerous cyber spiral
Sanctions and speeches won't stop the next blackout
POTUS DROPS THE RECEIPTS ON KREMLIN HACKERS 🧾🌐

social.text: naming moscow with all of NATO behind you actually bites.

choices.advisor: We look like grown-ups and keep the powder dry. Whether it deters the Kremlin is another matter.

choices.label: Absorb it quietly
choices.detail: Harden defenses, say nothing.
choices.result: You pour resources into defense and keep the attribution sealed. The lights come back on from Cleveland to Buffalo, but word leaks that you knew who did it and did nothing visible.

news.headline: President avoids escalation after crippling cyberattack
Silence after the blackout reads as weakness
No public response as grid attack goes unanswered
WHO TURNED OFF THE LIGHTS?? POTUS WON'T SAY 🤐🔦

social.text: toledo had no water for two days and the answer is... nothing?

choices.advisor: Cheapest path to peace, priciest path to looking helpless if this gets out.

title: Two for One
briefing: Tehran is holding a wrongfully detained American journalist and an aid worker, and will trade both for Farhad Nasseri, a convicted Quds Force spy serving life at ADX Florence. The Joint Chiefs hate freeing Nasseri; the families are in your outer office, crying. Make the swap and bring two innocents home, or refuse and hold the line on rewarding hostage-taking?

choices.label: Make the swap
choices.detail: Bring them home, free the spy.
choices.result: The exchange happens on a foggy tarmac in Doha at dawn. The journalist and aid worker walk into your arms on live TV; Nasseri boards a jet to Tehran, smirking. Critics warn every adversary now sees Americans as currency.

news.headline: President secures release of two detained Americans
Two innocents come home in Doha prisoner exchange
Trading a Quds Force spy invites more hostage-taking
TARMAC TEARS! POTUS SWAPS A SPY FOR OUR HEROES 😭✈️

social.text: she's been in Evin prison for 600 days. she's coming HOME. thank you.

choices.advisor: Two families whole again. Every cell block in Tehran just learned the price of an American.

choices.label: Refuse the deal
choices.detail: Hold the line, abandon the hostages for now.
choices.result: You decline and demand unconditional release instead. Tehran laughs and tightens the screws. The families go to the press; the photos of empty chairs at dinner tables are brutal.

news.headline: President refuses to reward Tehran's hostage diplomacy
Two Americans left in Evin as White House holds firm
Administration rejects spy swap, presses for release
POTUS SAYS NO DEAL AS HOSTAGES STAY CAGED 🔒💔

social.text: harsh, but you can't make every citizen a bargaining chip. respect.

choices.advisor: Principle intact, prisoners not. Those empty-chair photos will haunt the news cycle.

title: The Plan in the Papers
briefing: Operation Glasshollow, your classified contingency for a Chinese blockade of the Taiwan Strait, just appeared in full on the front page of the Tribune, sourced to someone inside your own Situation Room. The leak has tipped your hand to Beijing, Moscow, and Pyongyang at once. Launch a leak hunt that will paralyze your inner circle, or swallow it, rewrite the plan, and move on without a witch hunt?

choices.label: Order a full leak investigation
choices.detail: Polygraphs, phone records, the works.
choices.result: You unleash the FBI on your own staff. Polygraphs and seized phones turn the West Wing toxic; two aides resign in protest and the leaker stays hidden, for now.

news.headline: President hunts the traitor who exposed war plans
Leak probe turns the White House on its own staff
Sweeping investigation launched over Glasshollow leak
MOLE HUNT! POTUS POLYGRAPHS THE WHOLE WEST WING 🐭🔍

social.text: someone handed our Taiwan playbook to Beijing. find them.

choices.advisor: We might catch the leaker. We will definitely poison the room trying.

choices.label: Rewrite the plan, no manhunt
choices.detail: Cut losses, protect the team's trust.
choices.result: You order Glasshollow scrapped and rebuilt from scratch and spare your staff the inquisition. The Pentagon planners admire the calm; hawks fume that a leaker walked free.

news.headline: President scraps compromised plan, declines leak probe
President resists a paranoid purge over the leak
Letting a leaker walk free is a dangerous message
POTUS SHREDS THE PLAN, LETS THE MOLE SLIDE 📄✂️

social.text: boss took the hit instead of turning us into suspects. that's leadership.

choices.advisor: Trust preserved, mole at large. Sometimes the cheapest fix is a new plan and a short memory.

title: Stars Out of Line
briefing: Gen. Marcus Halloran, your Southern Command chief, has been quietly massing forces on the Colombian side of the Venezuelan border and telling officers he'll 'do what Washington won't.' It looks a step shy of insubordination, maybe worse. Relieve him publicly and risk fracturing a loyal officer corps, or rein him in privately and hope you've actually stopped him?

choices.label: Relieve him on the spot
choices.detail: Civilian control, made loud and clear.
choices.result: You fire Halloran in front of the cameras and reassert that generals do not freelance. The institutionalists exhale; his loyalists in the ranks seethe and a few resign in solidarity.

news.headline: President relieves general over unauthorized buildup
Civilian control upheld as rogue general is fired
Sacking a battlefield commander rattles the ranks
POTUS YANKS THE STARS OFF A ROGUE GENERAL ⭐🚪

social.text: a general who says he'll do what DC won't is a general who has to go. period.

choices.advisor: You just reminded everyone who's commander in chief. Some of the corps won't love the reminder.

choices.label: Rein him in privately
choices.detail: Quiet word, no public humiliation.
choices.result: You pull Halloran into a closed-door dressing-down and he stands down the buildup, sullenly. No headlines, but you can't be sure the ambition went away with the troops.

news.headline: President defuses general quietly, avoids a spectacle
Soft-glove handling of a general invites more defiance
Venezuela border buildup stood down after White House talks
WHAT HAPPENED BEHIND THAT DOOR? GENERAL GOES SILENT 🚪🤫

social.text: no spectacle, no martyr, no crisis. quiet competence, if it holds.

choices.advisor: Crisis avoided, ambition only paused. Watch that border, and watch him.

title: Ghosts in the Ballot
briefing: The DNI has caught a Chinese Ministry of State Security unit running a covert influence campaign and probing voter-registration databases in Georgia and Arizona ahead of the midterms. Going public could shake faith in the vote itself; staying quiet protects the system but leaves the public blind to the threat. Expose it now and risk the panic, or contain it behind closed doors and trust the agencies to plug the holes?

choices.label: Expose the operation publicly
choices.detail: Warn the country, brief the states.
choices.result: You take to the podium and lay it out. Georgia and Arizona rush to harden their systems; trust in the ballot wobbles as opponents seize on the word 'meddling' to question every result.

news.headline: President reveals Chinese interference targeting midterms
Transparency on election threat lets voters defend it
Sowing doubt about the vote does Beijing's work for it
BEIJING'S GHOSTS IN OUR ELECTIONS?! POTUS SOUNDS ALARM 🗳️👻

social.text: tell us the truth and let us fight it. sunlight beats a cover-up every time.

choices.advisor: Honesty arms the public and unsettles it. Expect the integrity of every close race to be questioned.

choices.label: Contain it quietly
choices.detail: Plug the holes without spooking voters.
choices.result: You order a silent scramble to lock down the state databases and counter the influence ops without a public word. The vote holds clean, but a later leak frames the quiet as a cover-up.

news.headline: President protects voter confidence amid foreign threat
White House hid a Chinese election attack from the public
Interference countered quietly, officials confirm later
POTUS KEPT THE ELECTION HACK A SECRET?! 🤐🗳️

social.text: they knew the whole time and said nothing. that's the part that scares me.

choices.advisor: Steady hands keep the vote intact. If 'we handled it quietly' ever leaks, it reads as 'we hid it.'

title: Tunnels Under Nogales
briefing: Intelligence has found a network of smuggling tunnels under Nogales, Arizona that the Sonora Cartel is using to move weapons and people, and Gov. Ray Delgado of Arizona is demanding troops on the line by morning. Deploying the military to the border projects strength but militarizes communities and unnerves Mexico City. Send the troops, surge surveillance instead, or hand it to civilian law enforcement and resist the spectacle?

choices.label: Deploy the military
choices.detail: Boots on the line, fast and visible.
choices.result: Convoys roll into Nogales by dawn. The tunnels go cold and Gov. Delgado praises you; border towns bristle at the checkpoints and Mexico City calls it heavy-handed.

news.headline: President sends troops to seal a smuggling corridor
Military on the border alarms communities and Mexico
Troops deployed to Nogales as cartel tunnels found
TROOPS STORM NOGALES, CARTEL TUNNELS GO DARK 🪖🕳️

social.text: finally someone takes the cartel tunnels seriously. send them.

choices.advisor: Strong optics, fast results. The checkpoints in people's hometowns are the price.

choices.label: Surge surveillance and sensors
choices.detail: Drones and sensors, no troops.
choices.result: You flood the Nogales corridor with drones, ground sensors, and tunnel-detection teams. The network gets mapped and slowly choked off, with far less spectacle, and far less instant payoff.

news.headline: President answers tunnel threat with surveillance surge
Sensors over soldiers eases fears of militarization
Drones are no substitute for boots on the line
POTUS UNLEASHES THE DRONE SWARM ON CARTEL TUNNELS 🛸🕳️

social.text: map the tunnels, choke the supply, skip the tanks on main street. smart.

choices.advisor: Quieter and cleaner, but Delgado wanted a parade and you gave him a spreadsheet.

choices.label: Keep it civilian
choices.detail: Let law enforcement own it.
choices.result: You hand Nogales to the FBI, DEA, and local police and decline to militarize. Mexico City and civil-liberties groups applaud; Gov. Delgado accuses you of leaving the border exposed.

news.headline: President keeps the border in civilian hands
No troops, no urgency: the border stays porous
Tunnel response handed to law enforcement, not military
POTUS SAYS NO TANKS, GOV. DELGADO LOSES IT 😤🚫🪖

social.text: the border is a police job, not a war zone. good call.

choices.advisor: Principled and patient. Delgado's outrage tour starts in three, two, one.

title: The Warning Nobody Read
briefing: A dock bombing at the Port of Savannah killed forty-one longshoremen and truckers, and a buried analyst's memo shows the agencies had the warning ten days early and sat on it. The DNI is offering you a quiet reorganization to avoid blame; an independent commission would air the failure in public and gut morale at Langley and Fort Meade. Order the open inquiry, or fix it internally and contain the damage?

choices.label: Convene an independent commission
choices.detail: Public accountability, painful but clean.
choices.result: You stand up a public commission with subpoena power. The hearings are brutal and Langley bleeds morale, but the country sees you chose truth over a cover-up.

news.headline: President orders public inquiry into Savannah failure
President demands accountability for ignored warning
Public flogging of the agencies hands the enemy a gift
POTUS RIPS THE LID OFF THE INTEL DISASTER 🔓💥

social.text: they knew. for ten days they KNEW. we deserve the whole truth.

choices.advisor: Bravest, bloodiest path. The hearings will hurt the spy shop for years, but the public trusts the cleanup.

choices.label: Reorganize quietly inside
choices.detail: Fix the wiring, spare the spectacle.
choices.result: You let the DNI run a discreet shake-up, reassigning the people who dropped the ball without a public reckoning. The agencies stabilize fast; the victims' families call it a whitewash.

news.headline: President steadies the agencies with a quiet overhaul
Forty-one dead in Savannah and the failure gets swept inside
Internal reorganization follows Savannah port attack
NO HEARINGS, NO NAMES: POTUS BURIES THE INTEL FAIL ⚰️📁

social.text: a 'quiet reorganization.' forty-one families and that's the answer. shameful.

choices.advisor: Fastest way to a functioning agency, surest way to a 'cover-up' headline. Choose your scar.
